Important secondary tags (dimensions): IPC: Communion/Warmth ↑, Agency ↔︎/↓; Big Five: Agreeableness/Extraversion overlap; SCM: Warmth ↑
Conceptual definition: Interpersonally welcoming, pleasant, and approachable (affiliation without necessarily helping).
Adjectives assigned: Affable, Agreeable, Companionable, Friendly, Good-natured, Hospitable, Kind, Kind-hearted, Neighborly, Pleasant, Warm, Welcoming, Approachable
Validation note (how this aligns with li: The IPC organizes interpersonal dispositions around Communion (warm–trusting vs cold–wary) and Agency (kennethlocke.org); these adjectives sit squarely on the warm/communion side. IAS-R work explicitly treats interpersonal adjectives as points in a circumplex (PubMed), and the IPC/“interpersonal circle” is closely tied to Big Five Extraversion & Agreeableness . Warmth is also a primary social-evaluative dimension in person perception (PubMed).

Conceptual definition: Emotionally supportive/helping stance: empathic concern, tenderness, understanding.
Adjectives assigned: Appreciative, Charitable, Concerned, Considerate, Cooperative, Empathetic, Gentle, Gentlehearted, Helpful, Sympathetic, Tender, Tenderhearted, Understanding
Validation note (how this aligns with li: DeYoung et al.’s BFAS separates Agreeableness into Compassion vs Politeness, and describes Compassion as “affiliat[ing] with others emotionally” and taking interest in others’ emotions —precisely the content of empathetic/tender/supportive terms. This cluster is also strongly “communion” in the IPC sense (kennethlocke.org).

Conceptual definition: Respectful, courteous, well-mannered conduct (rule-governed interpersonal consideration).
Validation note (how this aligns with li: BFAS Politeness contrasts respecting others vs pursuing one’s own desires “at the expense of others… even to the point of belligerence” , matching polite/courteous/respectful terms. This captures a normative/civility facet distinct from empathic Compassion .

Conceptual definition: Low interpersonal warmth/connection (distance, impersonal stance), not necessarily hostile.
Adjectives assigned: Aloof, Cold, Distant, Impersonal, Indifferent, Warmthless, Unsmiling
Validation note (how this aligns with li: IPC’s communion axis explicitly spans warm/trusting ↔ cold/wary (kennethlocke.org), and IAS-R conceptualizes interpersonal adjectives in that circumplex space (PubMed). These terms indicate low warmth (detachment) more than active antagonism, aligning with “cold” evaluations in person perception research on warmth (PubMed).

Conceptual definition: Low empathy/concern; harsh, unfeeling stance (often morally loaded).
Adjectives assigned: Callous, Coldhearted, Dissocial, Inconsiderate, Insensitive, Uncharitable, Unsympathetic
Validation note (how this aligns with li: These map to low Compassion (Agreeableness aspect) as defined by emotional affiliation/interest in others’ feelings . They also overlap with “socially malevolent” content (emotional coldness) described in Dark Triad work (Psychology Department - UBC), which Paulhus & Williams show shares low Agreeableness as a common correlate (Psychology Department - UBC).

Conceptual definition: Violations of civility/etiquette; overt interpersonal disrespect.
Validation note (how this aligns with li: This is the direct inverse of BFAS Politeness, which contrasts respect for others with belligerent/others-disregarding conduct . Keeping it separate from “cold” avoids mixing mere detachment with norm-violating antagonism.

Conceptual definition: Decisive, self-assured agency in interaction (taking initiative without necessarily controlling others).
Adjectives assigned: Assertive, Convinced, Decisive, Firm, Self-assured, Self-confident, Strong-minded
Validation note (how this aligns with li: IPC Agency spans confident/assertive ↔ meek/submissive (kennethlocke.org). BFAS explicitly includes an Assertiveness aspect of Extraversion , and Trapnell & Wiggins connect IAS-R dominance (DOM) to Extraversion measures , supporting an assertive/confident cluster.

Conceptual definition: High-control, force-imposing interpersonal stance (dominance used to direct others).
Validation note (how this aligns with li: IPC locates interpersonal styles as blends of agency and communion (kennethlocke.org) and trait descriptors often reflect factor blends rather than pure factors . “Domineering/overbearing” are classic high agency interpersonal descriptors; their coercive edge implies reduced communion (hence secondary tag).

Dark Triad theory explicitly notes socially malevolent tendencies including aggressiveness and emotional coldness (Psychology Department - UBC), and shows overlap with disagreeableness (Psychology Department - UBC), supporting bundling of aggression/cruelty/vindictiveness as a hostile-antagonistic cluster.

Important secondary tags (dimensions): IPC: Agency ↓; BFAS: Neuroticism–Withdrawal ↑; Social anxiety/low confidence
Conceptual definition: Low initiative due to inhibition/shyness/uncertainty (quiet, hesitant).
Adjectives assigned: Bashful, Indecisive, Inhibited, Passive, Shy, Timid, Unassertive, Doubting, Self-doubting
Validation note (how this aligns with li: IPC Agency runs from assertive to meek/submissive (kennethlocke.org). Trapnell & Wiggins note that adjectives like self-confident/self-assured vs shy/bashful relate to social confidence/anxiety and show relations with Extraversion and Neuroticism —supporting an inhibited/uncertain cluster distinct from principled “humility.”

Conceptual definition: Cooperative, accommodating submission/agreeing (yielding to others’ preferences).
Adjectives assigned: Accommodating, Agreeing, Meek, Submissive, Undemanding, Yielding
Validation note (how this aligns with li: IPC defines low agency as meek/submissive (kennethlocke.org); these adjectives add a compliant/agreeing behavioral flavor, consistent with locating them in low-agency interpersonal space rather than in “warmth” per se.

Important secondary tags (dimensions): IPC: Agency ↓ with low hostility; Approach–avoidance
Conceptual definition: Preference for avoiding confrontation/argument (low antagonistic activation).
Validation note (how this aligns with li: IPC’s axes allow “low hostility” stances to be represented as reduced cold/hostile content and/or lower agency (kennethlocke.org). This category is a pragmatic interactional style (avoidance) rather than a canonical lexical factor, so treat as a stance overlay on IPC position.

Conceptual definition: Transparent, straightforward, non-calculating orientation in dealing with others.
Validation note (how this aligns with li: HEXACO lexical work characterizes Honesty–Humility as capturing sincerity/fairness (honesty) and unpretentiousness/lack of greed (humility) (Differential Club), and Honesty–Humility includes facets like Sincerity and Fairness (projects.ori.org)—directly supporting “honest/guileless” grouping.

Adjectives assigned: Crafty, Cunning, Devious, Exploitative, Self-serving, Sly, Tricky, Wily, Calculating, Manipulative
Validation note (how this aligns with li: Paulhus & Williams define Machiavellianism as “the manipulative personality” (Psychology Department - UBC) and place duplicity/manipulation within socially malevolent tendencies (Psychology Department - UBC). HEXACO’s Honesty–Humility factor is explicitly about sincerity/fairness vs manipulative self-advancement (Differential Club), aligning these adjectives with low H.

Important secondary tags (dimensions): Big Five: Extraversion ↑; BFAS: Enthusiasm ↑ (and some Agency)
Conceptual definition: Outgoing, socially engaging, expressive interpersonal energy.
Adjectives assigned: Engaging, Extraverted, Gregarious, Magnetic, Outgoing, Sociable, Charismatic, Boisterous
Validation note (how this aligns with li: Big Five lexical structure robustly includes an Extraversion domain (projects.ori.org) and is summarized as a core dimension of trait organization (PubMed). BFAS includes an Enthusiasm aspect of Extraversion , which supports clustering expressive/engaging/gregarious terms (with “boisterous” as high activation–expressiveness).

Important secondary tags (dimensions): Big Five: Extraversion ↓; IPC: lower engagement; Behavioral avoidance
Adjectives assigned: Disengaged, Introverted, Reclusive, Unsociable, Withdrawn, Reticent, Silent, Taciturn
Validation note (how this aligns with li: The Big Five framework treats Extraversion as a basic dimension (and its reverse captures introversion/withdrawal) (PubMed). Trait descriptors frequently reflect blends but still organize strongly around these broad domains , making a single “withdrawn/introverted” bin defensible for these role labels.

Vigilance is commonly used to mean sustained attention/tonic alertness and is tied to arousal on the sleep–wake axis and cognitive performance (PMC), directly supporting grouping alert/attentive/engaged/focused terms. PANAS work also treats affect structure as systematic and tied to arousal/activation constructs (PubMed).

Conceptual definition: Reduced attentional control/clarity: distraction, unfocused, drowsy, “zoned out.”
Adjectives assigned: Bewildered, Confused, Distracted, Drowsy, Unfocused, Zoned out
Validation note (how this aligns with li: Vigilance is influenced by arousal/sleepiness, and vigilance assessment requires cognitive-function assessment (PMC). Sleep deprivation research shows vigilant attention is impaired by sleep loss and manifests as lapses/variability and “disorientation/cognitive impairment” phenomena around low-arousal states (PMC), consistent with drowsy/zoned out/distracted descriptors (with “confused/bewildered” reflecting reduced clarity).

Important secondary tags (dimensions): Affect: tense arousal ↓ (calm); Big Five: Emotional Stability ↑ (Neuroticism ↓)
Conceptual definition: Low reactivity and steadiness; calm stability rather than excitement.
Validation note (how this aligns with li: Activation research distinguishes tense arousal (tense–calm) from energetic arousal (PubMed), supporting a calm/steady cluster distinct from “positive-activated.” Trait-wise, this also aligns with Emotional Stability (inverse Neuroticism) as a Big Five dimension (PubMed).
